{"step_by_step_thinking": "The child has ingested a 10% fluoride solution, which is a high concentration of fluoride. Immediate action should aim to reduce the absorption of fluoride into the bloodstream. Drinking copious quantities of water can help dilute the fluoride in the stomach and promote its excretion through urine. While milk or calcium-containing liquids can bind to fluoride and reduce its absorption, the immediate action should focus on dilution. Sending the child to the family physician is a secondary step if symptoms persist or worsen. Therefore, the most effective immediate action is to have the child drink copious quantities of water.", "answer_choice": "A"}
